title: XT-8
content:
{{Top|ncl}}
{{Unlicensed}}
[[File:XT8-Challenge66.png|thumb|right|XT-8]]
'''XT-8''' was a [[XT labor droid]] which had been modified to be an [[Assassin droid/Legends|assassin droid]]. He was painted a dull green and given [[Photoreceptor/Legends|photoreceptors]] which glowed with an evil, red light as well as a [[Vocabulator/Legends|vocabulator]] which produced a buzzing, barely coherent voice. XT-8's personality matrix was crude even for an assassin droid by the time of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], though the XT model was high technology around [[20,000 BBY/Legends|20,000 BBY]]. During combat, XT-8's chest panel would swing apart to reveal a [[submachine]] gun which could fire 180 degrees to the front.

XT-8 was part of the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Leshy Drobo]]'s crew and helped him capture [[Roni von Wasaki]]. While Drobo and [[Gammid]] provided a distraction, XT-8 sneaked up behind Wasaki and stunned him. Once Drobo had loaded Wasaki onto the ''[[Starkiller]]'', now frozen in [[Carbonite/Legends|carbonite]], XT-8 was tasked with piloting the ship to receive the bounty. However, Wasaki managed to influence Drobo to free him through use of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. XT-8 soon found out about this and confronted Drobo in the engine room, firing on him and killing him while also damaging the [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]]. This caused the ship to drop to [[Realspace/Legends|realspace]] and the journey that would have taken but 30 days in [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] now dragged on for over 20,000 years. During all this time, XT-8 thought only of delivering his cargo.

Sometime during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], however, a ship containing a group of [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebels]], one of them being a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]], dropped out of hyperspace near the ''Starkiller''. When they attempted to dock XT-8 fired upon them but, as the ships had over 20,000 years between their construction, the ''Starkiller'' was easily overpowered. The group then proceeded to board the ship and, when they entered the cockpit, were fired upon by XT-8. The group then disabled the droid, ending his mission once and for all.
